rulings.Case Title: DAVID DURETTE SR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 29, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of producing evidence for defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to prove its case, rather, the record reflects that plaintiff may have difficulty doing so given that not all Victor products contained asbestos. The Court grants the motion as to the false representation and punitive damages. The burden shifted and plaintiff has not presented evidence of a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CAROLAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 29, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL CAROLAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 29, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC'S Amended Motion For Summary Judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery responses and the declaration of Wrobel. Defendant's identification of witnesses was devoid of any facts as to what the witnesses knew or contact information. Here plaintiff had contact information for at least one witness, Mr. Speck. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact. Defendant's objections to the Declarations of Speck and Ay are sustained based on Thoren v. Johnston & Washer (1972) 29 Cal. App. 3d 270 as to the Speck Declaration. Defendant has established the underlying facts for the Court to find the failure to disclose what Mr. Speck knew and where he resided was on bad faith. Further, Mr. Speck's subsequent deposition testimony demonstrates that he lacks the knowledge to establish that Mr. Carolan was exposed to asbestos from defendant's products. Ay's testimony is based on Speck's declaration and may not be used to establish the existence of the control fact at issue, contact with a Leslie product containing asbestos. =(302/PJM/)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG YATES VS. UNION SQUARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer For Abatement Of Action (Ccp 430.10(C); Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 29, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO UPTOWN PARKING CORPORATION'S Demurrer For Abatement Of Action (CCP 430.10(C)) is overruled, 20 days to answer. Under Leadford v. Leadford (1992) 6 Cal. App. 4th 571, 575, it appears that the Court lacks the authority to abate the action. Although the federal case is in California, the federal court's jurisdiction differs. Defendants request is properly addressed by a motion to stay. This case warrants a single assignment. Within 1 week, the parties are to advise this Court's clerk of the judge or judges whom the parties would like the case assigned to. If the parties are unable to reach agreement, they are to advise the clerk of that. The Presiding Judge will make the assignment and advise the parties. =(302/PJM/AY)
